sections.title: The Subjunctive Mood (संभावनार्थक)
sections.explanation: The subjunctive mood in Hindi expresses wishes, possibilities, doubts, suggestions, and hypothetical situations — things that may or may not happen. It is formed by using a special set of verb endings that differ from the indicative (factual) mood. The subjunctive endings are: मैं -ऊँ (e.g., जाऊँ), तू -ए (जाए), तुम -ओ (जाओ), वह/यह -ए (जाए), हम -एँ (जाएँ), आप -एँ (जाएँ), वे/ये -एँ (जाएँ). The subjunctive is used after certain constructions: शायद (perhaps) — शायद वह आए (Perhaps he may come); चाहना कि (to want that) — मैं चाहता हूँ कि तुम जाओ (I want you to go); ज़रूरी है कि (it is necessary that) — ज़रूरी है कि वह पढ़े (It is necessary that he study). The subjunctive never takes the auxiliary है/हैं — the verb form alone expresses the mood.
sections.tip: The subjunctive form for तुम (-ओ) looks identical to the familiar imperative (तुम जाओ). Context tells you whether it is a command or a subjunctive wish. After शायद, कि, or in conditional clauses, it is subjunctive.

examples.hi: मैं चाहता हूँ कि तुम मेरे साथ चलो।
examples.en: I want you to come with me.

examples.hi: ज़रूरी है कि हम समय पर पहुँचें।
examples.en: It is necessary that we arrive on time.

examples.hi: भगवान करे सब ठीक हो जाए।

exercises.prompt: "शायद वह कल _____" — सही रूप चुनें। (Choose the correct form for: Perhaps he may come tomorrow.)
exercises.explanation: After शायद (perhaps), the subjunctive form is used. For वह, the subjunctive of आना is आए. The indicative आता है would state a fact, and the future आएगा would express certainty — but शायद expresses uncertainty, requiring the subjunctive.

exercises.prompt: संभावनार्थक (subjunctive) में सहायक क्रिया है/हैं का प्रयोग होता है या नहीं? (Is the auxiliary है/हैं used in the subjunctive?)
options: नहीं, सहायक क्रिया नहीं लगती (No, no auxiliary is used)
exercises.explanation: The subjunctive mood in Hindi never takes the auxiliary है/हैं. The bare subjunctive verb form alone expresses the mood: शायद वह आए (not *शायद वह आए है). This is one of the key distinguishing features of the subjunctive.

exercises.prompt: "मैं चाहता हूँ कि वह _____" — रिक्त स्थान में 'पढ़ना' का कौन सा रूप आएगा? (Fill in the blank with the correct form of पढ़ना.)
exercises.explanation: After चाहना कि (to want that), the subjunctive is required. For वह, the subjunctive of पढ़ना is पढ़े. The construction 'मैं चाहता हूँ कि + subjunctive' is one of the most common uses of the subjunctive in Hindi.

sections.title: Imperative Forms (आज्ञार्थक) — तू, तुम, आप
sections.explanation: Hindi has three levels of imperative (command) forms corresponding to the three second-person pronouns, each carrying a different level of formality and politeness. The तू form (intimate/rude): uses the bare verb stem — तू जा (Go!), तू बैठ (Sit!), तू खा (Eat!). This form is used with very close friends, children, animals, or to show anger/disrespect. The तुम form (informal/friendly): adds -ओ — तुम जाओ (Go), तुम बैठो (Sit), तुम खाओ (Eat). This is the standard informal imperative used with friends, peers, and younger people. The आप form (formal/respectful): adds -इए/-इये — आप जाइए (Please go), आप बैठिए (Please sit), आप खाइए (Please eat). This is the polite imperative used with elders, strangers, and in formal situations. There is also an extra-polite form using -इएगा: आप बैठिएगा (Would you please sit) which adds a future tense marker to soften the request further.
sections.tip: When in doubt about which form to use, always default to the आप form (-इए). It is never wrong to be too polite in Hindi. Using तू with the wrong person can be deeply offensive.

examples.en: Would you please look at this.

exercises.prompt: "बैठना" का आप-स्तर का आज्ञार्थक रूप क्या है? (What is the आप-level imperative of बैठना?)
exercises.explanation: The आप-level imperative adds -इए to the verb stem: बैठ + इए = बैठिए. This is the polite form. बैठो is the तुम-level (informal), and बैठ is the तू-level (intimate/rude).

exercises.prompt: अगर आप किसी अजनबी से बात कर रहे हैं, तो कौन सा रूप उचित है? (Which form is appropriate with a stranger?)
exercises.explanation: With strangers, you should always use the आप form (बैठिए) to show respect. Using तुम or तू with a stranger would be considered rude or overly familiar. Hindi social norms require formal address with people you don't know well.

exercises.prompt: "-इएगा" प्रत्यय किस लिए प्रयोग होता है? (What is the -इएगा suffix used for?)
options: अतिरिक्त विनम्रता के लिए (For extra politeness)
क्रोध व्यक्त करने के लिए (To express anger)
exercises.explanation: The -इएगा suffix creates an extra-polite request by adding a future tense marker to the imperative. It softens the command into a gentle request: बैठिएगा (Would you please sit) sounds more courteous than even the already polite बैठिए (Please sit).

sections.title: Polite Requests & Prohibitions
sections.explanation: Beyond the basic imperative forms, Hindi has several constructions for making polite requests and expressing prohibitions. For polite requests, you can use: कृपया (please, formal) — कृपया बैठिए (Please sit); ज़रा (just/a bit, softener) — ज़रा सुनिए (Just listen for a moment); क्या आप...सकते हैं? (Can you...?) — क्या आप दरवाज़ा खोल सकते हैं? (Can you open the door?). For prohibitions (negative commands), Hindi uses मत (don't) with तू/तुम forms and न with आप/subjunctive forms: तुम मत जाओ (Don't go, informal); आप न जाइए (Please don't go, formal); मत रोओ (Don't cry). The word मत is placed directly before the verb. For stronger prohibitions, you can use नहीं with the future tense: तुम वहाँ नहीं जाओगे (You will not go there — stern prohibition). Note that मत is used exclusively with imperatives, while नहीं is the general negation word.
sections.tip: To soften any request, add ज़रा (just/a little) before the verb: ज़रा बताइए (Just tell me) sounds much gentler than बताइए (Tell me). Combining कृपया and -इए is the most polite form possible: कृपया बताइए (Please do tell).

examples.hi: तुम वहाँ मत जाओ, ख़तरनाक है।
क्या आप मेरी मदद कर सकते हैं?
examples.en: Can you help me? (polite request as question)

exercises.prompt: "मत" और "न" में क्या अंतर है निषेधात्मक आज्ञा में? (What is the difference between मत and न in negative commands?)
options: मत तू/तुम के साथ, न आप/संभावनार्थक के साथ (मत with तू/तुम, न with आप/subjunctive)
दोनों एक ही हैं (Both are the same)
मत औपचारिक है, न अनौपचारिक (मत is formal, न is informal)
exercises.explanation: मत is used for direct prohibitions with तू/तुम imperatives: तुम मत जाओ (Don't go). न is used with आप forms and in subjunctive contexts: आप न जाइए (Please don't go), or ऐसा न करें (One should not do this). Using the right one matches the formality level.

exercises.prompt: सबसे विनम्र अनुरोध कौन सा है? (Which is the most polite request?)
exercises.explanation: कृपया ज़रा बताइएगा combines three politeness strategies: कृपया (please), ज़रा (just/a bit, softener), and -इएगा (extra-polite imperative). This makes it the most courteous option. बता दे is the तू form (very direct) and बता दो is the तुम form (informal).

exercises.prompt: "ज़रा" शब्द का अनुरोध में क्या कार्य है? (What role does ज़रा play in a request?)
options: यह अनुरोध को नरम बनाता है (It softens the request)
यह आदेश को कठोर बनाता है (It makes the command harsher)
यह प्रश्न बनाता है (It forms a question)
exercises.explanation: ज़रा literally means 'a little' or 'just', and when placed before an imperative, it softens the request by implying it will only take a small effort or moment: ज़रा सुनिए (Just listen for a moment) feels much gentler than सुनिए (Listen).
